heroHeadline: DeepSeek V4 Launches on Huawei Ascend — China's AI Stack Goes Independent; Auto China 2026 Showcases Flying Cars, Humanoid Robots, In-House AI Chips
heroSubtitle: DeepSeek V4 launched April 24 with native Huawei Ascend NPU support and 73% API cost reduction — completing China's domestic AI stack one day after Lutnick confirmed zero Nvidia H200 chip purchases; Tencent and Alibaba in talks to invest in DeepSeek at $20B+ valuation; Auto China 2026 opens with Xpeng unveiling flying car (2027), IRON humanoid robot (Q4 2026), and GX Level 4 robotaxi with in-house 3,000 TOPS Turing chips; SMIC and Hua Hong shares surge 9–15% on DeepSeek V4 news
footerNote: Data sourced from MIIT, BIS, Bloomberg, FT, SCMP, CSET, Nikkei Asia, TechInsights, DeepSeek, Huawei Annual Reports, TrendForce, US Trade Representative, CBP, China MOFCOM, Stanford HAI AI Index 2026, House Select Committee on CCP, CNN, NPR, Al Jazeera, TechCrunch, Tom's Hardware, DigiTimes, Semafor, MIT Technology Review, CNBC, Associated Press, Yahoo Finance, PR Newswire
